SUBJECT: Request For Formal Advice
This is a request for formal advice in my capacity as
Executive Officer of the Franchise Tax Board. The Franchise
Tax Board is a statutory public agency authorized by
Government Code Section 15700. The members of the Franchise
Tax Board are the State Controller (currently Gray Davis),
the chairman of the State Board of Equalization (currently
Brad Sherman) and the Director of Finance (currently Thomas
Hayes).
Backg round
Each year the staff of the Eranchise Tax Board prepares
income tax booklets which contain instructions and tax
return forms for taxpayers, These booklets are printed by
the State Printer. 
The booklets are mailed automatically to
taxpayers who filed state returns the prior year, to
taxpayers $/ho request the booklets and other persons vrho did
not file prior year tax returns, but as to vrhom the
Franchise Tax Board has obtained information suggesting a
possible filing requirement. Most booklets are mailed
without being requested by the recipients. 
The booklets are
prepared for individuals who are California residents (Form
!aOl; inaividuals who are nonresidents (Porm 54ONR); and
individuals srho can use various short form returns (Form
540A, Form 54OEz). Booklets are also prepared, printed and
mailed to corporate taxpayers and others (Form I00, Eorm
54I, etc. )
The bookl-ets are prePared annually and contain information'
instructions and forms' pertinent to the tax year
involved. The cover of ine booklet indicates which booklet

contained in the booklet, the official 
State Seal and
reference to the State of California Franchise Tax Board.
(A complete copy oE the 1991 California Resident Income Tax
Form and Instruction Booklet is attached. )
